description_long: This object is a large, irregularly shaped stone block formation with a rugged, natural rock texture and earthy brown coloration. The block is arranged in a U-shaped or horseshoe configuration, partially enclosing a landscaped area with visible greenery and a paved path leading into the center. The stone surfaces are uneven, with prominent crevices, protrusions, and a weathered appearance, suggesting exposure to the elements. The interior of the U-shape contains a dense cluster of small trees or shrubs, and the block appears to serve as a substantial barrier or retaining structure. The stone's surface is matte and rough, with no visible artificial markings or engravings. This type of structure is commonly found in urban parks or landscaped public spaces, serving both functional and aesthetic purposes.
description: A large, U-shaped stone block barrier with a rough, natural rock texture, partially enclosing a landscaped area with greenery and a paved path. The surface is rugged and weathered, typical of urban park or garden landscaping.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, rugged stone face with the U-shaped enclosure partially visible and some greenery peeking over the top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the stone block and the transition from the stone barrier to the landscaped area with visible trees and a paved path.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the open end of the U-shape, showing the dense cluster of trees and the central paved path leading into the enclosed area.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the stone's rough surface and the integration with the landscaped area, with more greenery visible along the edge.
attribute_location_context: urban park or landscaped public garden
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of weathering with rough, uneven textures and some minor chipping, but no significant moss or water erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous barrier enclosing a landscaped area
